info.name: CoffeeShop POS - Full API (Professional)
info.description: Full API test collection generated from current NestJS routes in api-v1.

request.description: Login with the seeded Super User account (phone: 0999999999). Use the returned token in Bearer auth to access all /api/erp/* endpoints.
Returns all coupons including assigned_user info, usage_count, usage_limit, and expires_at.

body.raw:
{
  "code": "SUMMER20",
  "discount_percent": 20,
  "is_active": true,
  "note": "Summer campaign — applies to all Coffee items",
  "usage_limit": 100,
  "expires_at": "2026-12-31T23:59:59.000Z",
  "assigned_user_ids": [],
  "menu_ids": [],
  "category_ids": [1]
}

{
  "auto_generate_code": true,
  "discount_percent": 10,
  "is_active": true,
  "note": "Personal welcome coupon — specific menu items only",
  "usage_limit": 1,
  "expires_at": "2026-12-31T23:59:59.000Z",
  "assigned_user_ids": [{{customerId}}, 2, 3],
  "menu_ids": [1, 2],
  "category_ids": []
}

request.description: Creates a coupon restricted to specific customers. assigned_user_ids accepts multiple user IDs. Omit or pass [] to make it available to everyone.
Partially updates a coupon. assigned_user_ids replaces the full list — pass [] to open it to everyone, omit the field to leave assignments unchanged.
Returns all meeting rooms with their type, capacity, status, and price.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Boardroom A",
  "description": "Main boardroom with projector and whiteboard",
  "capacity": 20,
  "price_per_hour": 50.00,
  "type": "conference",
  "status": "available",
  "notes": "Includes AV equipment and high-speed Wi-Fi"
}

request.description: type: standard | vip | conference | executive. status: available | maintenance | inactive.
Blocked if the room has pending or confirmed bookings.
Returns available: true/false plus any conflicting bookings for this room.

query.description: pending | confirmed | cancelled | completed (omit for all)

request.description: Accessible by ADMIN and CASHIER roles. Filter by status query param.
Allowed transitions: pending → confirmed | cancelled; confirmed → completed | cancelled. Confirming creates a Google Calendar event automatically.
Cancelling a confirmed booking also removes the Google Calendar event.
Returns all 15 coffee rank tiers ordered by tier number.
Edit the label, min_points, or icon for a rank tier. All fields are optional — send only the ones you want to change.
Returns all rewards (coupon and item types) configured for the given tier.

item.name: Rank Tier Rewards - Add Coupon Reward

body.raw:
{
  "type": "coupon",
  "label": "10% Off Welcome Coupon",
  "description": "Enjoy 10% off your next order as a thank-you for reaching this tier.",
  "coupon_discount_percent": 10,
  "coupon_expires_days": 30,
  "is_active": true
}

request.description: type: coupon — requires coupon_discount_percent. coupon_expires_days (optional) sets how many days the auto-generated user coupon is valid.

item.name: Rank Tier Rewards - Add Item Reward

body.raw:
{
  "type": "item",
  "label": "Free Signature Latte",
  "description": "A complimentary Signature Latte on us!",
  "menu_id": {{menuId}},
  "quantity": 1,
  "is_active": true
}

request.description: type: item — requires menu_id. quantity defaults to 1.
Partially update a reward. All fields are optional. Toggling is_active=false hides it from new rank-ups without deleting issued rewards.
Permanently removes a reward from the tier. Already-issued user_rank_rewards are not affected.
Returns coupons that are active, not expired, and below their usage limit. Also shows assigned_user_id so cashier knows if a coupon is user-restricted.

header.description: Optional — prevents duplicate order on retry

request.description: coupon_code is optional. customer_id is required only when the coupon has an assigned_user_id.
Generates a dynamic KHQR string for the order. Returns qr (display to customer), md5 (for polling), and expires_at. Order status → awaiting_payment.
Poll this endpoint every 3–5 seconds while showing the QR. Automatically checks Bakong API for confirmed payment. Returns order_status and bakong_transaction_status (pending → success).
Generates a Baray pay-link for the order. Returns url (redirect/display to customer) and expires_at. Order status → awaiting_payment.
Poll every 3–5 seconds while showing the Baray pay link. Returns order_status and baray_transaction_status (pending → success).

Cashier manually confirms the customer scanned a bank QR on the table (ABA, ACLEDA, Wing, Pi Pay, etc.). No gateway required — creates a SUCCESS payment transaction immediately. Use this as a fallback when Bakong/Baray is unavailable.

exec: console.log('QR Table payment recorded. Transaction ID:', json.data.id, '| Bank:', json.data.note);

header.description: Optional — prevents duplicate order on retry

request.description: coupon_code is optional. The API validates expiry, usage limit, and user assignment automatically.
Returns coupons the authenticated customer is allowed to use: active, not expired, below usage limit, and either public or assigned to this customer.
Generate a Baray pay-link for a wallet top-up. Returns url and expires_at.
Poll payment state for a wallet deposit (works for both Baray and Bakong channels).
Generate a KHQR QR code for a wallet top-up. Returns qr, md5, and expires_at.
Cancel all pending KHQR wallet deposit intents so a new one can be generated.

item.name: Wallet - Bakong Abandon Deposit by ID

request.description: Cancel a specific pending KHQR wallet deposit intent.

item.name: Rewards - My Profile (balance + rank + badge + history)

request.description: Returns all rank-up rewards issued to the authenticated customer. Each entry shows the reward type (coupon / item), status (pending / claimed / expired), the tier it was earned from, and — for coupon rewards — the auto-generated coupon code and its expiry.
Mark a pending item reward as claimed. Use the id from the My Rank Rewards list. Coupon rewards are auto-activated on rank-up and do not require a separate claim step.
Returns a base64 PNG data URI QR code encoding the customer profile URL. Generated once and cached on the user record.

header.description: Optional — prevents duplicate payment initiation on retry

request.description: Generate a Baray pay-link for the order. Customer opens URL to complete bank payment.
Poll every 3–5 seconds while showing the Baray pay link. Scoped to the order owner.
Generate a KHQR QR code for the order. Returns qr, md5, and expires_at.
Poll every 3–5 seconds while showing the KHQR. Scoped to the order owner.
Cancel the pending KHQR for this order so a new QR or different payment method can be used.

request.description: Creates a booking with status=pending. Admin/cashier must confirm it. Conflict check prevents double-booking.
Returns all bookings belonging to the authenticated customer.
Customer can cancel their own pending or confirmed booking. Completed bookings cannot be cancelled.
No auth required. Returns all rooms whose status is 'available'.
No auth required. Returns every available room annotated with is_available: true/false for the requested date range.

item.name: 11) ERP — Super User
item.description:
All endpoints require a JWT with SUPER_USER role (role_id: 4). Login via Auth - Login as Super User first.

Base path: /api/erp/

Modules:
  payroll/     → Employees, Attendance, Leave, Payroll periods
  purchasing/  → Suppliers, Purchase Orders, Goods Received
  pl/          → Expense Categories, Operating Expenses, P&L Report
  recipe-costing/ → Auto product cost calculation
  analytics/   → Business intelligence dashboard

request.description: Returns all employees with linked user profile (name, phone, email).
Links an existing system user as an ERP employee. contract_type: full_time | part_time | contract | internship.

Creates or updates today's attendance record. hours_worked is auto-calculated from clock_in/clock_out.
status: present | absent | late | half_day | holiday | on_leave.
Leave types: annual | sick | unpaid | maternity | paternity | other.
Returns all payroll periods with their line-item totals.
Full breakdown per employee: base_salary, overtime_pay, leave_deduction, net_salary.

Auto-calculates salary for ALL active employees.

Formula per employee:
  overtime_pay   = overtime_hours × hourly_rate × 1.5
  leave_deduction= (unpaid_leave_days / working_days) × base_salary
  net_salary     = base_salary + overtime_pay − leave_deduction
Locks the payroll (draft → finalized). Once finalized it is included in P&L reports.
Marks finalized payroll as paid (finalized → paid).
Returns supplier with last 10 purchase orders.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Fresh Dairy Co.",
  "contact_person": "Mr. Sovan",
  "phone": "0123456789",
  "email": "sovan@freshdairy.com",
  "address": "Phnom Penh, Cambodia",
  "payment_terms": "Net 15 days",
  "notes": "Primary milk supplier"
}

request.description: Purchase Total = Σ(Item Cost × Quantity). Auto-generates PO number (PO-{timestamp}).
Status flow: draft → ordered → partial → received. Or draft/ordered → cancelled.
Records goods received and auto-updates ingredient stock quantities and unit_cost. PO status → partial or received.
type: fixed | variable

Full Profit & Loss statement.

Formulas applied:
  Revenue        = Σ(order amounts)
  COGS           = Σ(ingredient cost × qty used)
  Gross Profit   = Revenue − COGS
  Gross Margin % = (Gross Profit / Revenue) × 100
  OpEx           = Σ(operating expenses in period)
  Payroll Cost   = Σ(net_salary from finalized payrolls)
  Net Profit     = Revenue − (COGS + OpEx + Payroll Cost)
  Net Margin %   = (Net Profit / Revenue) × 100

item.name: Recipe Costing - All Menus with Cost

request.description:
Returns every menu with auto-calculated cost metrics and recipe completeness status.

Per menu (no-size): cost, price, profit, food_cost_pct, margin_pct, can_produce, status.
Per size (has_sizes): same fields per S/M/L variant.

Status values:
  complete        — recipe configured and all ingredient costs set
  missing_recipe  — no recipe lines configured
  missing_cost    — recipe exists but ≥1 ingredient has unit_cost = 0
  inactive        — menu is_available = false

Formulas:
  Cost          = Σ(ingredient_quantity × unit_cost)
  Profit        = price − cost
  Food Cost %   = (cost / price) × 100
  Margin %      = ((price − cost) / price) × 100
  Can Produce   = min(floor(stock / qty_per_serving)) across all ingredients

Configuration health dashboard cards.

Returns:
  total_products           — total menu count
  recipes_complete         — menus with status = complete
  missing_recipes          — menus with no recipe configured
  missing_ingredient_costs — menus with recipe but missing unit costs
  inactive                 — menus not currently available
  avg_food_cost_pct        — average food cost % across priced items
  avg_margin_pct           — average gross margin % across priced items
  highest_margin           — { menu_name, margin_pct }
  lowest_margin            — { menu_name, margin_pct }

Full ingredient-level cost breakdown for one menu.

Per ingredient: name, unit, quantity_used, unit_cost, line_cost, stock_on_hand, can_produce_from_this.
Per size (has_sizes): same per S/M/L variant with its own ingredient list.

Also returns: cost, price, profit, food_cost_pct, margin_pct, can_produce, status.

Cost trend over time for one menu.

Returns list of { size, cost, recorded_at } snapshots ordered chronologically.
Use POST /snapshot to record a new data point.

Copies the recipe from source menu into target menu.

For sized menus: copies per-size recipes matching by size enum (S/M/L).
For no-size menus: copies the flat recipes array.

Both menus must use the same size mode (has_sizes must match).
Useful for cloning: Hot Latte → Iced Latte without rebuilding the recipe manually.
Single-request ERP dashboard: financials + best sellers + sales trend + peak hours + waste analysis.
Ranked by total quantity sold. Returns menu name, total_qty, total_revenue.
Orders grouped by hour of day. Reveals busiest and slowest hours for staffing decisions.

Per-menu: revenue, COGS, gross_profit, margin_pct.
Formula: Profit Margin = (Net Profit / Revenue) × 100

Ingredient waste records with waste percentage.
Formula: Waste % = (Waste / Total Inventory) × 100
Baray payment gateway webhook. Register this URL in the Baray dashboard. Excluded from JWT auth.
